Huang Gai, styledGongfu, was fromQuanling[泉陵],Lingling[零陵].A
TheBook of Wustates: He was a descendant of the formerGrand AdministratorofNanyang, Huang Zilian [黃子廉]. His branch split off and his ancestor moved toLingling[零陵], where the family settled.[Huang] Gaiwas orphaned when he was young, suffered from the deaths of his relatives, and experienced hardships in their fullest. Yet, he had great aspirations, and though he was poor, he was not the same as ordinary people, for he often learned how to write letters and studied about matters of warfare when he had free time from carrying firewood on hisback.1•••
Here, “carrying firewood on his back” [負薪] is a metaphor for a very humble life.↩
He first served as an official for his commandery, then was nominated asFilial and Incorruptand recruited into the bureau of theExcellencies. WhenSun Jianwas mustering righteous troops,[Huang] Gaifollowed him.[Sun] Jiandefeated the hill bandits in the south and he chased offDong Zhuoin the north.[Huang] Gaiwas conferred asMajor of a Separate Unit. When[Sun] Jiandied,[Huang] Gaifollowed[Sun] Ceand[Sun] Quan, putting on armor, marching about, treading on blades, and massacring cities.
In counties where the various Shanyue were not submissive and they faced enemies and trouble,[Huang] Gaiwould always be appointed as the actingChiefthere. The officials ofShicheng county[石城縣] especially found it troublesome to bring constraint and control, so[Huang] Gaiappointed twobureaucratsthat each had control over the bureaus. He ordered:
“I am lacking in virtue, and it was only because of my military exploits that I became an official; I cannot be commended of having civil talents. As of now, the rebels and enemies have not been pacified, and there is still business pertaining to military activity, so I will have all civil documentation entrusted to the twobureaucrats. They shall inspect and audit the bureaus to examine and uncover any mistakes and errors. Whatever these twobureaucratssign will enter as documents and leave as approvals.2However, if there is any misdoing from them, I will never inflict any whippings or beatings. They should each work wholeheartedly and not act towards being ahead of the multitude.3”•••
Specifically, “approvals” [諾] refers to the Hanzi character for it being signed at the end of a document to express permission for the document to be implemented by the superior officer. This is saying thatHuang Gaiwould approve any documentation that the two officials forwarded.↩
Huang Gaiis saying that they should not try to benefit themselves to be better than the average person.↩
They were first both in fear of his authority, so they were respectful in their duties day and night, but after a long time, the officials believed that[Huang] Gainever looked at the civil documentation, so they gradually become too lenient with others in many matters.[Huang] Gaiwas also suspicious that the outside was becoming lax and lazy, so he occasionally made inspections. From each of them, he got that the twobureaucratswere not upholding the law for several matters. He thus invited all the officials, gifted them wine and meals, then used the opportunity to question them about the matters. The twobureaucratswere inarticulate and they all kowtowed and apologized for their crimes.[Huang] Gaisaid, “Previously, I gave the command that I would never inflict any whippings or beatings. That was no lie.” He killed them. The county was terrified. Later, he was transfered to becomeChiefofChungu[春穀長] andPrefectofXunyang[尋陽令]. He governed nine counties in total, and so there was peace and order in them. He was promoted toChief CommandantofDanyang[丹楊都尉]. He curbed the strong and supported the weak, and so the Shanyue willingly submitted.
[Huang] Gaiwas stern and unyielding in his demeanor, and he was fond of training soldiers. Each time he was on a military expedition, the soldiers all strived to be at the front. During theera, he followedZhou Yuto resistExcellency CaoatChibi[赤壁], and he proposed the strategy to attack them with fire. This is spoken of in theBiography of [Zhou] Yu.BHe was conferred asHousehold General of Military Edge.
TheBook of Wustates: At the battle ofChibi[赤壁之役],[Huang] Gaiwas hit by flying arrows. At the time, it was cold, and he fell into the water. He was retrieved byWusoldiers, but they did not know he was[Huang] Gaiand placed him on a bed in a side room.[Huang] Gai, with force, shouted once to call forHan Dang, and when[Han] Dangheard him, he said, “This is the voice ofGongfu!” He shed tears facing him and took off and changed his clothes. Thus, he survived.
The Man barbarians ofWuling[武陵] rebelled, attacking and defending cities.[Huang] Gaiwas thus made theGrand Administrator. At the time, the commandery only had five hundred soldiers, and he could not match the foe with them. Thus, he shut the city gates. When the rebels were halfway in, he would then strike them. He beheaded hundreds and the rest of them all fled and returned to their settlements. He executed the leaders and pardoned the followers. From spring to summer, all the enemies and rebels were pacified, and the marquises, lords, and chiefs of the deep mountains and the Ba [巴水], Li [醴水], You [由水], and Dan rivers [誕水] all changed their conduct and altered their ambition, presenting gifts and requesting meetings. The commanderies were thus cleansed. Later,Yiyang county[益陽縣] inChangsha[長沙] was attacked by the mountain bandits, but[Huang] Gaialso pacified and suppressed them. He was madeDeputy General. He died in office.
[Huang] Gaiundertook his office with decision, and there were no matters that piled up. The people of the state missed him.CWhen[Sun] Quanascended the throne steps, he remembered his achievements, so he conferred his son,[Huang] Bing, with peerage ofMarquis within the Passes.
TheBook of Wustates: The likeness of[Huang] Gaiwas also drawn, and offered sacrifices for each season of the four seasons.

Huang Gai, styled Gongfu, was a man from Chuanling in Lingling.AHe started off as a minor clerk in the commandery office, but then he was recommended as a “Filial and Incorrupt”.1When Sun Jian recruited a volunteer force, Huang Gai followed him. Sun Jian went on to defeat the Shanyue bandits in the south and sent Dong Zhuo fleeing in the north; he then made Huang Gai Major with a Separate Command. After Sun Jian died, Huang Gai followed Sun Ce and Sun Quan, fighting here and there, doing battle in the fields and conqueringcities.•••
The Han administration filled its offices through local recommendation of talented people. “Filial and Incorrupt” is one of the categories which one can be recommended for. Naturally, the official criteria for recommendation for this title are filial piety and upright behaviour.
To fight against Dong Zhuo.↩
FromHistory of Wu: He was the descendent of Huang Zilian, former Grand Administrator of Nanyang. The branches of his family were scattered, and since his grandparents moved to Lingling, Huang Gai’s family had lived there since. Huang Gai was orphaned at an early age, and endured all kinds of difficulties even when he was just a baby. However, he was full of great ambitions, and even though he was in a poor situation, he did not content himself in being like the ordinary rather, when he was not carrying firewood, he spent his free time learning how to read and write, and discussing military affairs.
The Shanyue people were insurgent, and Huang Gai was assigned to oversee the troubled prefectures. However, the officers in Shicheng Prefecture were insubordinate and difficult to control. Thus Huang Gai appointed two officers as heads of the departments. He said to them thus, “Being the county’s magistrate, I am neither talented nor virtuous. I obtained this post based on military merits, and administrative affairs are not my specialty. At this time, the bandits have not been pacified, and I have military duties to fulfil. Thus, I delegate the two of you to be in charge of all the documents, to supervise the various departments, and to point out as well as correct any mistake that they make. Within the jurisdictions of your two offices, you should discharge your responsibility for your administrative duties properly. Though I will neither punish you with whip nor cane, I hope that you will perform your duties to the best of your abilities, and not set a bad example for the rest.” At first, all were in fear of him, and night and day they took their tasks seriously. After a while, though, the officers, seeing that Huang Gai did not check their documents, became more relaxed and more lenient.
Huang Gai was unhappy with their lazy outlooks, and at some point in time, some inspections were carried out, and he obtained evidence of the two officials’ unlawful activities. And so he held a gathering and invited all the officers to wine and food, and during the meal, he brought out the evidence and interrogated them about it. The two department heads had nothing to say in their own defence, and kowtowing, they begged for forgiveness. Huang Gai said, “Some time ago, I had already set out the decree—‘Nor whip nor cane will I lay on you’. That was not said in vain.” Thus he had them executed. All within the prefecture trembled.
Later, he was transferred to be Chief of Chunju and Prefect of Xunyang. In all, he had been put in charge of nine prefectures, and wherever he was, the land became peaceful. In time he was promoted to be Colonel of Danyang, and there he received popular support from the Shanyue because he put down the powerful and supported the weak.
Huang Gai’s appearance was stern and determined, and he was good at training soldiers. Every time he went on a military expedition, his troops would fight to go first. During of the Jian’an reign, he followed Zhou Yu to defend against Lord Cao at Chibi. He suggested the stratagem of fire-attack. This is recorded in Zhou Yu’s biography.BHe was made “Martial-sharp” General of the Interior. When the southern tribes in Wuling rebelled and started to take over cities and towns, Huang Gai assumed the duties of the Grand Administrator [of one of the commanderies]. He only had five hundred men with him in the city. Knowing that they would lose in a simple attack, he had the city gates opened. When half of the bandits were in the city already, he struck, and in that battle several hundred enemies were beheaded. The rest fled, and returned to their home towns. Huang Gai defeated the leader of the rebels, and pardoned all those who surrendered. Between spring and summer, all the rebellions were quelled, and the chiefs from Ba, Li, You, and Dan who were supporting the rebels changed their ways. Bearing gifts, they asked for pardon, and everywhere within the boundaries of the commendary was peaceful again. Later on, Yiyang prefecture in Changsha was attacked by Shanyue bandits, and so Huang Gai went to fight them again. After that he was promoted to Lieutenant-General. He died of illness while in office.
FromHistory of Wu: During the Chibi battles, Huang Gai was hit by a stray arrow, and fell into the river in full winter cold. He was saved by some soldiers of Wu, but they didn’t know that it was Huang Gai. They placed him lying in a bed. Huang Gai gathered enough strength to cry out Han Dang’s name, and when Han Dang heard it, he said, “That’s the voice of Gongfu!” Thus he turned to him, and weeping, he assisted him to change his wet clothes. And so Huang Gai survived.
Huang Gai was decisive and determined as an officer, and did not tarry on any of his tasks. People in the kingdom remembered him fondly.CWhen Sun Quan assumed the throne, he considered Huang Gai’s past merits, and made his son Huang Bing a Marquis of the Interior.2•••
A Marquis of the Interior is not given a fief, but salaried directly from the imperial court.↩
FromHistory of Wu: They also drew Huang Gai’s likeness and offered sacrifices to him all year long.
